pages.raw_text:
Says a Spokane union labor
official:


The efforts of President Com
pers of the American Federation of
Labor to interest union labor in
things political and to secure bet
ter treatment at the hands of the
politicians OF the country is meet
ing with fair portion of success.
The greatest obstacle Mr.. Gompers
has to contend with is the lack
of interest evidenced by members
of organized labor in politics. For
some reason the union men fail to
register and participate in nomin-
ating the tickets and seem to con-
tent themselves with sitting back
and complaining or in letting some
one else make the fight for them.


This state of affairs was we'll
illustrated during the last week.
when poll of one Spokane union
meeting, with 100 members pres
ent, showed that only two of those
present had registered. Another
polled last Wednesday
night showed out of 60 present only
tire registered. 1t is this great
lack of interest on the part of the
unionists that will make the work
of the officers of the American
Federation most difficult"
